$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports verbatim.
TypeScript port; behaviour byte-equivalent to upstream.
Full 14-entry deprecation chain (v1..v14) ported. v10..v14 share a helper renderer (renderModernSave) to keep the file readable; markup is equivalent.
Upstream image/video/group transforms ported; added bidirectional core/cover ↔ artisanpack/cover block transforms. cleanEmptyObject from unlock(blockEditorPrivateApis) is inlined.
TypeScript port; behaviour byte-equivalent to upstream.

TypeScript port. The dependency on ../embed/util.matchesPatterns is replaced by an inline matchesPatterns helper so the fork does not depend on block-library internals not exposed via the package's exports field.
TypeScript port; single 'cover' variation preserved verbatim.
TypeScript port of the main CoverEdit component. Uses sibling fork modules instead of block-library deep imports.
TypeScript port. Uses inline CoverInserterIcon instead of @wordpress/icons.cover so the editor canvas does not have to load dashicons.css.
TypeScript port. cleanEmptyObject from unlock(blockEditorPrivateApis) is inlined.

TypeScript port. Inlines cleanEmptyObject. Replaces private ResolutionTool with a plain SelectControl in a ToolsPanelItem. Replaces private HTMLElementControl with a plain SelectControl rendered under InspectorControls group='advanced'. Replaces ../utils/poster-image with an inline MediaUploadCheck + MediaUpload pair. Drops useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps (../utils/hooks) so the optional dropdown menu is omitted.

TypeScript port. Upstream wraps the private ResizableBoxPopover API; the fork wraps a plain ResizableBox so it does not depend on lock-unlock block-library internals. The popover is rendered in-flow instead of via the popover slot.

Sass @use references to @wordpress/base-styles/{colors,variables,z-index} inlined: $black → #000, $white → #fff, $content-width → 840px, $grid-unit-30 → 24px. @for loops expanded to literal CSS rules for has-background-dim-{10..100}.
Inline SVG for the inserter (matches @wordpress/icons cover path).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; upstream init.js / index.php server-side wiring is intentionally absent.

knownDivergences: transforms.ts and inspector-controls.tsx / block-controls.tsx inline cleanEmptyObject rather than importing it from unlock(@wordpress/block-editor.privateApis). Practical impact: none — semantics are equivalent.

edit/resizable-cover-popover.tsx uses ResizableBox in-flow rather than the private ResizableBoxPopover. Practical impact: the resize handle renders inline under the block instead of as a slotted popover; resize behaviour itself is unchanged.

edit/inspector-controls.tsx replaces the private ResolutionTool with a plain SelectControl wrapped in a ToolsPanelItem. Practical impact: the same options/value/onChange surface is presented, but without the upstream tool's panel-id wiring.

edit/inspector-controls.tsx replaces the private HTMLElementControl with a plain SelectControl rendered in InspectorControls group='advanced'. Practical impact: the tagName select is identical; the upstream tool's extra a11y affordances (label associations) are not duplicated.

edit/inspector-controls.tsx replaces ../utils/poster-image with an inline MediaUploadCheck + MediaUpload pair. Practical impact: the user can pick a poster image; the upstream-only 'remove poster' affordance is omitted (use the panel's reset-all to clear).
embed-video-utils.ts inlines matchesPatterns rather than importing from ../embed/util. Practical impact: none — the inline implementation matches upstream byte-for-byte.
Cover does NOT vendor a Caption component (the block doesn't use one in upstream).
